steps.title: Set up the cosine ratio
steps.content: In a right triangle, $\cos\theta=\dfrac{\text{adjacent}}{\text{hypotenuse}}$. From the figure, identify the side adjacent to the angle $x^\circ$ and the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle).

steps.title: Plug in and compute
steps.content: Using the side lengths shown in the figure, form the ratio $\cos x^\circ = \dfrac{\text{adjacent}}{\text{hypotenuse}}$. Dividing the adjacent length by the hypotenuse length gives approximately $0.3928$.
